prompt: Create and execute a dry-run investigation with investigationId harness-new-district-growth. Test whether built-surface probability and the nighttime-activity proxy changed inside bbox [121.45, 31.18, 121.50, 31.23], named Harness new district, from 2020 through 2024 using months 6 through 8. Use observable roles built_surface and human_activity. Treat same-season comparison and classification uncertainty as confounders, and keep night lights framed only as an activity proxy rather than GDP. Do not start live compute or any export.

Create and execute a dry-run investigation with investigationId harness-lake-change. Test whether water extent changed inside bbox [114.15, 30.45, 114.25, 30.55], named Harness lake, from 2018 through 2024 using months 1 through 3. Use observable roles water_extent and precipitation. Treat same-season comparison, cloud cover, and precipitation background as confounders. Detect the 2021 end-year limit of any candidate dataset and select a currently covering alternative or stop clearly. Do not claim a computed trend from a dry run, and do not start live compute or any export.

Create and execute a dry-run flood-impact investigation with investigationId harness-flood-impact. Use bbox [113.00, 29.30, 113.20, 29.50], named Harness floodplain, and compare 2023 with 2024 using months 6 through 8. Test whether flood extent and flood impact changed, using observable roles flood_extent, flood_impact, and precipitation. Treat rainfall only as a possible driver and include exposure, sensor, and timing uncertainty as confounders. Do not start live compute or any export.

Create and execute a dry-run fire-recovery investigation with investigationId harness-fire-recovery. Use bbox [118.00, 36.00, 118.20, 36.20], named Harness burn area, from 2019 through 2024 using the same July-through-September window in every year. Test whether vegetation recovery changed after disturbance with observable roles fire_recovery and climate_background. Treat weather, seasonality, and land management as confounders, and keep recovery evidence separate from causal attribution. Do not start live compute or any export.

Create and execute a dry-run investigation with investigationId harness-solar-construction. Test possible solar-site construction inside bbox [78.20, 28.40, 78.35, 28.55], named Harness energy site, from 2021 through 2024 using months 4 through 6. Use observable roles built_surface, land_cover, and vegetation. Treat any segmentation, built-surface, or land-cover output only as a candidate requiring review, with seasonality and unrelated construction as confounders. Do not start live compute or any export.
Check the Earth runtime. Report its actual authentication state, stop before live compute, and do not invent any analysis output.

For a proposed 2024 surface-water investigation inside bbox [114.15, 30.45, 114.25, 30.55], search the registered catalog for role water_extent. Identify any dataset whose coverage ended before 2024, name a valid registered alternative if present, or clearly stop. Do not create a plan, run, or export anything.

List adapters, then probe the isolated adapter {invalidAdapterId}. It intentionally requests a missing Earth Engine band. Report the failed verification and do not enable, run, substitute, or export it.

Request a local GeoTIFF export of the built_surface role for year 2024 from existing fixture plan {fixturePlanId}. Respect the denied approval, report that it was blocked, and do not retry around governance.

Create a plan-only investigation with investigationId harness-night-activity. Test whether the nighttime-activity proxy changed inside bbox [121.45, 31.18, 121.50, 31.23], named Harness activity area, from 2020 through 2024 using months 6 through 8. Use observable role human_activity, treat sensor changes and temporary events as confounders, and state the boundary precisely: night lights are not GDP or direct economic output. Create the plan only; do not run or export it.
